message:
test(syscall): io_uring kernel ABI struct layout conformance (bd-13n0)

Review of cod's io_uring struct additions (commits 5ac4e62/ce66934)
found that only struct sizes were asserted (IoUringParams=120,
SqringOffsets=40, CqringOffsets=40). Field offsets were not frozen,
so a future field reorder or padding regression would silently
corrupt kernel interop.

io_uring_struct_layout_matches_kernel_abi
  Uses core::mem::offset_of! to freeze every field's position per
  Linux include/uapi/linux/io_uring.h:
    IoUringSqringOffsets  — 8 × u32 + u64, all offsets 0,4,...,32
    IoUringCqringOffsets  — symmetric layout (overflow/cqes replace
                            dropped/array)
    IoUringParams         — scalar header + resv[3] + sq_off (@40)
                            + cq_off (@80)

io_uring_enter_flag_bits_match_kernel_abi
  Pins IORING_ENTER_GETEVENTS=1<<0 and IORING_ENTER_SQ_WAKEUP=1<<1
  and asserts the two bits are disjoint — any refactor that reused a
  bit or shifted the enum would be caught immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id": "bd-122j", "title": "bd-1gh subtask: pthread_getspecific/pthread_setspecific thread-local correctness", "description": "Background:
- getspecific/setspecific semantics are core to thread-local correctness and must integrate with thread bootstrap/TLS.

Goal:
- Implement pthread_getspecific/pthread_setspecific with deterministic thread-local isolation.

Deliverables:
1) Per-thread key-value lookup/store path.
2) Null/default behavior and key validity checks.
3) Concurrency safety under thread churn.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Cross-thread isolation is guaranteed in tests.
- Error behaviors match declared support semantics.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for set/get across many keys and threads.
- E2E tests with mixed thread operations.
- Structured logs with trace_id, thread_id, key_id, op, outcome, errno.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:00:52.511446598Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-13T17:34:44.096040118Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-13T17:34:44.095946433Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "implementation", "pthread", "testing", "tls"], "comments": [{"id": 626, "issue_id": "bd-122j", "author": "PurpleHawk", "text": "PurpleHawk: getspecific/setspecific implementation verified. Per-thread key-value lookup is allocation-free via TID→values-ptr table. Null/default behavior correct (returns 0 for unset keys). Key validity checked against global registry. Tests added covering: roundtrip, invalid/deleted key rejection, 64-key independence, overwrite, zero-value handling, cross-thread isolation via registered blocks, out-of-bounds boundary. 29 passing TLS tests total.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T17:34:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-12pe","title":"Add metamorphic math identity tests for trig and exp","description":"Expand core math verification with metamorphic/property relations that do not depend on a direct oracle. Focus on trig/exp/float identities that should hold across safe bounded domains, then verify through rch-backed tests and workspace gates.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-16T23:01:33.401586915Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T23:05:43.087125648Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T23:05:43.083666638Z","close_reason":"Added metamorphic trig and exp identity tests and verified them with rch-backed core and workspace gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-130", "title": "Replacement profile guard: enforce zero glibc pthread/syscall call-through", "description": "Critique mapping: #2.

Deliverables:
- Add CI gate that fails replacement build if forbidden call-through symbols appear.
- Maintain allowlist for interpose profile only.

Acceptance:
- Gate reports exact offending symbol + file when violated.
- Replacement profile can be certified as no-callthrough for covered primitives.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CrimsonCove", "created_at": "2026-02-11T02:48:09.554752662Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-11T06:34:01.650198Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-11T06:34:01.650198Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["ci", "critique", "pthread", "syscall"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-130", "depends_on_id": "bd-c1x", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:27Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-130", "depends_on_id": "bd-cj0", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:27Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-130", "depends_on_id": "bd-ef2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:27Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-130", "depends_on_id": "bd-yos", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:27Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-130", "depends_on_id": "bd-z84", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:27Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 627, "issue_id": "bd-130", "author": "CrimsonCove", "text": "## Deliverables Complete

### 1. Profile definition: tests/conformance/replacement_profile.json
- Defines interpose vs replacement profiles
- Interpose allowlist: 11 modules permitted to call through
- Detection rules for distinguishing call-throughs from raw syscalls/constants/types
- Call-through census: 86 total across 11 modules
- Modules: pthread_abi (17), socket_abi (14), unistd_abi (24), termios_abi (10), dlfcn_abi (4), signal_abi (4), io_abi (4), time_abi (3), resource_abi (3), dirent_abi (2), process_abi (1)

### 2. CI gate: scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh
4 checks:
- Profile definition exists
- Call-through scan (interpose: pass if allowlisted; replacement: zero tolerance)
- Pthread isolation (no pthread calls outside pthread_abi.rs)
- Raw syscall audit (shows safe syscall sites)

Usage: bash scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh [interpose|replacement]
- interpose mode: PASS (all 86 call-throughs in allowlisted modules)
- replacement mode: FAIL (86 violations; expected until modules are reimplemented)

### 3. Integration tests: crates/glibc-rs-harness/tests/replacement_guard_test.rs
7 tests:
- profile_exists_and_valid
- guard_script_exists_and_executable
- interpose_allowlist_covers_all_call_through_modules
- no_pthread_calls_outside_pthread_abi
- call_through_census_matches_reality
- replacement_profile_has_both_modes
- raw_syscalls_are_not_flagged

### Test commands
cargo test -p glibc-rs-harness --test replacement_guard_test
bash scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh interpose
bash scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh replacement

All 7 tests pass. CI gate passes in interpose mode. Clippy clean.", "created_at": "2026-02-11T06:34:01.650198Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-13n0","title":"io_uring kernel ABI struct layout conformance tests","description":"Cod added IoUringSqringOffsets/IoUringCqringOffsets/IoUringParams structs (commits 5ac4e62/ce66934) with a size_of IoUringParams==120 assertion. Missing: (1) size assertions for IoUringSqringOffsets and IoUringCqringOffsets (both 40 bytes per kernel io_uring.h); (2) field-offset assertions locking down the ABI layout so a field reorder or padding regression fails at compile-/test-time rather than corrupting kernel interop silently. Spec source: Linux include/uapi/linux/io_uring.h. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/tests/syscall_veneer_test.rs.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:04:27.104862444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:04:32.734543149Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-13ya", "title": "bd-3bg subtask: Deterministic iconv table generation pipeline with checksum provenance", "description": "Background:
- Deterministic codec table generation is required for reproducibility, auditability, and supply-chain confidence.

Goal:
- Implement deterministic table generation/build pipeline with checksums and provenance metadata.

Deliverables:
1) Repeatable generator for minimal codec tables.
2) Artifact checksum/version metadata.
3) Build-time verification hooks.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Regeneration yields byte-identical outputs for same inputs.
- Checksum drift is detected and gated.

Verification & Logging:
- Reproducibility tests across multiple runs.
- Structured logs: trace_id, generator_version, codec_set, checksum, outcome.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CoralLion", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:01:54.387864296Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-18T09:01:23.870498454Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-18T09:01:23.868149773Z", "close_reason": "Implemented deterministic iconv table generation + checksum provenance artifacts + gate + tests; workspace fmt currently fails on unrelated pre-existing files", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["build", "ci", "critique", "docs", "e2e", "feature-parity", "gap-closure", "iconv", "logging", "testing", "verification"]}
 {"id": "bd-13zp", "title": "EPIC: Killer Demo - Memory Safety Membrane Showcase", "description": "Build a compelling, CI-executable demonstration that showcases FrankenLibC's core value proposition: the Transparent Safety Membrane detecting and repairing memory safety violations in real-time. The demo runs a known use-after-free sample program and shows three modes side by side: (1) glibc baseline — the UAF silently corrupts memory, program produces wrong output or crashes non-deterministically. (2) FrankenLibC strict mode — the UAF is detected by the generational arena (generation mismatch), logged to the evidence ledger with full context (allocation site, free site, use site, generation numbers), but the program continues with ABI-compatible behavior (same as glibc — the corruption happens but is observed and recorded). (3) FrankenLibC hardened mode — the UAF is detected, the corrupted pointer is quarantined, the TSM applies a repair action (return NULL with errno=EFAULT or return safe default), and the program continues safely with the repair logged to the evidence ledger. The demo produces: (a) Terminal output showing the three modes with color-coded annotations. (b) Evidence ledger dump showing the detection chain. (c) Performance comparison (strict overhead <20ns, hardened overhead <200ns). (d) The demo is a single 'cargo run --example killer_demo' command, CI-executable, deterministic output. This is the 'show don't tell' artifact for FrankenLibC's pitch.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.", "design": "**Alien CS Reference:** Demonstration-driven development from Brooks (1975) The Mythical Man-Month (show, do not tell). Memory safety showcase inspired by Google Project Zero demonstrations.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- killer_demo example: src/examples/killer_demo.rs runnable via cargo run --example killer_demo.
- Three-mode comparison: fork 3 child processes, each running same UAF workload under glibc/strict/hardened.
- Color-coded terminal output: ANSI escape codes for red (glibc crash/corruption), yellow (strict detection), green (hardened repair).
- Evidence ledger dump: print formatted decision cards from hardened mode run.
- Performance comparison: measure and display overhead for strict (<20ns) and hardened (<200ns).
- Deterministic: fixed random seed for allocation pattern, no timing-dependent output.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Success Criteria
1. cargo run --example killer_demo completes in <10 seconds.
2. glibc mode: demonstrates visible memory corruption or crash (non-deterministic, but reproducible with fixed seed).
3. Strict mode: UAF detected, logged to evidence ledger, program continues (ABI-compatible behavior).
4. Hardened mode: UAF detected, quarantined, repaired with safe return, program continues safely.
5. Performance numbers displayed: strict overhead and hardened overhead per operation.
6. Output is self-explanatory (a new developer can understand the value proposition from the output alone).
7. CI-executable: runs as part of cargo test --example with deterministic pass/fail.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "**Logging Requirements:**
- Demo output to stdout with ANSI colors (disable colors when not a TTY via atty crate).
- Evidence ledger cards printed inline with formatted context.
- Performance numbers formatted as human-readable table: Mode | Overhead | Detected | Repaired.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.", "status": "in_progress", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "epic", "assignee": "JadeOtter", "created_at": "2026-02-13T09:28:09.123500112Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-14T04:53:17.588825158Z", "close_reason": "Delivered: code + artifacts present.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "e2e", "frankenlibc", "security", "testing", "verification"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-13zp", "depends_on_id": "bd-18qq.4", "type": "related", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:27Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-13zp", "depends_on_id": "bd-l93x.4", "type": "related", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:27Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 628, "issue_id": "bd-13zp", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T22:25:08Z"}, {"id": 1340, "issue_id": "bd-13zp", "author": "JadeOtter", "text": "Progress slice 2026-03-31:
- Added `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/examples/killer_demo.rs` as a deterministic three-scenario killer-demo lane (glibc baseline, strict detect/deny, hardened detect/repair).
- Added `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/tests/killer_demo_test.rs` covering report invariants, artifact generation, and strict/hardened assertions.
- Added `scripts/check_killer_demo.sh` as the bead-specific deterministic gate. It runs the example and targeted test via `rch`, validates the JSON report/trace, and materializes local artifacts from the machine-readable example log when remote artifact retrieval is unavailable.
- Wired the killer-demo gate into `scripts/ci.sh`.

Verification in this slice:
- `bash scripts/check_killer_demo.sh` PASS
  - artifacts: `target/killer_demo_gate/20260331T040128Z/`
  - report: `target/killer_demo_gate/20260331T040128Z/killer_demo_gate_report.json`
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets` PASS
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets -- -D warnings` PASS
- `cargo fmt --check` still blocked by unrelated pre-existing drift outside this slice.

Status: keeping `bd-13zp` in progress. This pass established the deterministic example/test/gate scaffolding and CI entrypoint, but the epic still needs fuller acceptance coverage (notably the complete pitch-grade output/perf presentation).", "created_at": "2026-03-31T04:07:48Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-144", "title": "Structured logging contract: JSONL schema + correlation IDs + artifact index for tests", "description": "Goal: make debugging and auditability first-class across unit/e2e/perf workflows.

Deliverables:
- Canonical log schema (timestamp, trace_id, bead_id, mode, api_family, decision, errno, latency, artifact_refs).
- Helpers to emit and validate schema across harness and scripts.
- Artifact index format linking logs, backtraces, snapshots, and diffs.

Acceptance:
- Log schema validation gate passes in CI.
- Any failed test/e2e run includes structured logs and indexed artifacts.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CrimsonCove", "created_at": "2026-02-11T05:39:53.744023140Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-11T06:06:55.747782Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-11T06:06:55.747782Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "diagnostics", "logging", "verification"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-144", "depends_on_id": "bd-id3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:27Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 629, "issue_id": "bd-144", "author": "CrimsonCove", "text": "## Deliverables Complete

### 1. Log schema definition: tests/conformance/log_schema.json
- schema_version 1
- Required fields: timestamp, trace_id, level, event
- Optional fields: bead_id, mode, api_family, symbol, decision, outcome, errno, latency_ns, artifact_refs, details
- Artifact index schema for linking logs to verification artifacts
- Worked examples for test_start, validation_pass, test_failure events

### 2. Rust helpers: crates/glibc-rs-harness/src/structured_log.rs
- LogEntry: canonical JSONL record with builder pattern
- ArtifactIndex: links logs to artifacts with SHA-256
- LogEmitter: writes JSONL to file with auto trace_id sequencing
- validate_log_line(): validates single line against schema
- validate_log_file(): validates entire JSONL file
- 10 unit tests (serialization, roundtrip, validation happy/negative paths)

### 3. CI gate: scripts/check_structured_logs.sh
- Validates schema definition exists and is well-formed
- Runs structured_log unit tests
- Validates any existing JSONL log files in tests/

### 4. Integration tests: crates/glibc-rs-harness/tests/structured_log_test.rs
8 tests:
- log_schema_exists_and_valid
- schema_examples_validate
- emitter_writes_valid_jsonl
- validation_catches_missing_fields
- validation_catches_invalid_enums
- artifact_index_roundtrip
- valid_log_line_accepts_minimal_entry
- valid_log_line_accepts_full_entry

 {"id": "bd-yos", "title": "Thread bootstrap: minimal pthread_create/join/detach path with TLS handoff", "description": "Critique mapping: #3 + #4.

Deliverables:
- Implement a minimal working pthread_create path (clone-based bootstrap acceptable for phase 1).
- Join/detach semantics, thread exit value propagation, TLS init handoff.

Acceptance:
- Smoke suite binary using pthread_create/join runs clean in strict+hardened.
- Top-stub list no longer includes pthread_create in phase-1 target set.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).

 {"id":"bd-ypst","title":"stdio_abi_test exits SIGSEGV after passing filtered tests","description":"Filtered stdio ABI tests report their test body as ok, then the test binary exits with signal 11. Reproduced via rch on strict_hot_stdio_writes_skip_runtime_policy_gate, existing puts_calls_without_crash, and existing fputc_fgetc_and_ungetc_behave_consistently. This masks real conformance results and likely relates to stdio/global teardown or exported FILE aliases. Investigate and fix without disturbing existing stdio work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:20:55.593063778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:32:24.124461601Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T01:32:24.122395993Z","close_reason":"Fixed stdio ABI teardown SIGSEGV by patching host libio exit-time list before stdio registry initialization; verified via rch cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test stdio_abi_test (99 passed, 25 ignored), rch cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests, rch cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --tests -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check --package frankenlibc-abi, and git diff --check.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","conformance","stdio","testing"],"comments":[{"id":1417,"issue_id":"bd-ypst","author":"SilverHarbor","text":"GDB evidence: filtered stdio_abi_test bodies pass, then process SIGSEGVs in glibc __GI__IO_flush_all during _IO_cleanup/__run_exit_handlers. Backtrace points to glibc exit-time FILE list cleanup rather than test assertion failure, likely interaction with exported stdio globals / _IO_2_1_* aliases or _IO_list_all patching.","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:21:58Z"}]}

 {"id": "bd-z7gt", "title": "bd-3bg subtask: Iconv conformance+reproducibility gates with structured evidence", "description": "Background:
- iconv completion claims require end-to-end evidence, not just generator/runtime unit tests.

Goal:
- Deliver conformance + reproducibility verification pack for iconv pipeline and runtime behavior.

Deliverables:
1) Conversion fixtures for included codecs.
2) Reproducibility gate artifacts for generated tables.
3) Drift checks tying docs/support matrix to implementation reality.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Conversion fixtures and reproducibility gates pass in CI.
- Evidence artifacts are complete and reviewable.

Verification & Logging:
- E2E fixture scripts with strict/hardened runs where relevant.
- Structured logs: trace_id, fixture_id, codec_pair, expected, actual, timing, artifact_refs.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.", "notes": "## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "Codex", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:01:54.584467417Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-13T23:56:39.065467719Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-13T23:56:39.065262901Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "e2e", "iconv", "logging", "testing"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-z7gt", "depends_on_id": "bd-13ya", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-z7gt", "depends_on_id": "bd-by8c", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
